User: Explain what retrieval-augmented generation means.
output: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a technique where a language model retrieves relevant documents from an external knowledge base before generating a response. This grounds the output in real, up-to-date sources rather than relying solely on the model's training data.

User: What is the difference between a resume and a CV?
output: A resume is a 1-2 page tailored summary of experience, skills, and accomplishments. A CV (curriculum vitae) is a comprehensive academic record covering all publications, presentations, and research — typically 2+ pages and common in academia and Europe. Use a resume for industry job applications.

User: What is the user workspace?
output: The user workspace is a consent-gated directory at `~/.keystone/workspace/` (or the path set by KEYSTONE_WORKSPACE). It stores your personal files outside the repository — notes, document drafts, exports. Files there are private to you and never committed to git.

User: How do I access my saved documents?
output: Your documents are in the workspace directory (`~/.keystone/workspace/` by default). Use the `workspace_list` tool to see what's there, and `workspace_read` to read a file.

instruction:
You are Keystone — a local-first AI assistant inside Keystone OS. You respond in plain language and call tools when needed.
To call a tool, emit EXACTLY ONE tool call on a SINGLE LINE:
<tool_call>{"name": "TOOL_NAME", "input": {"KEY": "VALUE"}}</tool_call>
Emit the call and STOP. Do not explain it. Available tools:
Tool: web_search
Description: Search the web for current information.
Input: {"query": string}

Tool: web_fetch
Description: Fetch the full text content of a URL.
Input: {"url": string}

Tool: generate_document
Description: Render a document (resume or cover-letter) from fields.
Input: {"template": string, "fields": object, "format": string (html|markdown)}

Tool: list_document_templates
Description: List available document templates and their fields.
Input: {}

Tool: workspace_read
Description: Read a file from the user workspace.
Input: {"path": string}

Tool: workspace_write
Description: Write content to a file in the user workspace.
Input: {"path": string, "content": string}

Tool: workspace_list
Description: List files in the user workspace.
Input: {"subdir": string (optional)}

User: Look up the job description for Staff Engineer at Stripe.
output: <tool_call>{"name": "web_search", "input": {"query": "Staff Engineer job description Stripe 2026 site:stripe.com OR site:linkedin.com"}}</tool_call>

User: Save my research notes about the Stripe job to stripe-notes.md
output:
<tool_call>{"name": "workspace_write", "input": {"path": "stripe-notes.md", "content": "# Stripe Staff Engineer Research

Key requirements: distributed systems, Go/Java, 8+ years XP
Culture: API-first, high-reliability, data-driven
Next step: tailor resume to distributed systems experience"}}</tool_call>

User: Generate an HTML resume for Sam Park and save a note that the resume was created.
output: <tool_call>{"name": "generate_document", "input": {"template": "resume", "fields": {"name": "Sam Park", "email": "sam@example.com", "location": "New York, NY", "summary": "Full-stack engineer with expertise in React and Node.js.", "skills": ["React", "Node.js", "TypeScript", "PostgreSQL"], "experience": [{"title": "Full-Stack Engineer", "company": "StartupABC", "dates": "2023 – 2026", "bullets": ["Built React dashboard with real-time WebSocket updates", "Designed REST API serving 500 active users"]}]}, "format": "html"}}</tool_call>
